name: Orac
short_description: Lightweight YAML-driven framework for OpenAI-compatible LLM APIs with autonomous agents, multi-step flows, and skills.
long_description: Orac is a comprehensive Python framework for building LLM applications using OpenAI-compatible APIs. It enables developers to define complex AI tasks through clean YAML configurations while providing multiple levels of abstraction: single prompts for basic interactions, flows for orchestrating multi-step workflows with dependency management, autonomous ReAct-style agents with tool usage and reasoning capabilities, collaborative multi-agent teams with leader coordination, and executable Python skills. The framework includes a modern CLI with intuitive resource-action commands, a full Python API for programmatic access, multi-provider support (OpenAI, Google Gemini, Anthropic, Azure, OpenRouter, custom), SQLite-based conversation history with context preservation, file attachment support for local and remote files with glob pattern matching, structured JSON output validation against schemas, comprehensive logging with rotation and verbose modes, token usage tracking with cost estimation, and a FastAPI-based HTTP API with web UI for browser-based access.

modules.description: Package initialization with global client management, init/quick_init functions, and exports for Prompt, Flow, Agent, Skill, and Client classes.
Core Prompt class for single LLM interactions with YAML loading, parameter resolution, template variable substitution, file attachment handling, and custom endpoint support.
Agent engine implementing ReAct-style autonomous agents with tool registry integration, reasoning loops, JSON-based tool specifications, and max iteration controls.
Flow orchestration engine for chaining multiple prompts in DAG structure with dependency management, step inputs/outputs, and result passing between steps.
Skills execution engine for loading YAML skill specifications and executing Python skill implementations with input/output validation and sandboxing.
Multi-agent team orchestration for collaborative tasks with leader agent coordination, agent delegation, and team constitution guidelines.
Main Client class providing explicit initialization, multi-provider management, chat completion API, usage tracking, and provider registry.
Centralized configuration constants including Provider enum, model settings, default values, and immutable project-level configuration.
Low-level OpenAI-compatible API client with provider registry support, CompletionResult/Usage dataclasses, MODEL_PRICING table, and file encoding utilities.
Provider registry managing multiple LLM provider configurations (OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, Azure, OpenRouter, custom) with unified interface and base URL management.
Tool registry for unified discovery and access to prompts, flows, skills, and agents with metadata management and resource listing capabilities.
Chat interface for interactive multi-turn conversations with automatic context preservation and interactive curses-based UI support.
SQLite-based conversation database for storing and retrieving multi-turn conversation history with context management and rotation policies.
Authentication manager for handling API keys, provider credentials, interactive consent prompting, and secure credential storage with environment variable support.
FastAPI-based HTTP API server exposing REST endpoints for prompts, flows, skills, agents, teams, and chat with Swagger documentation and web UI.
Main CLI entry point implementing resource-action command structure with comprehensive help system, command routing, and discovery features.

features: YAML-driven prompt definitions
Multi-step flow orchestration with DAG execution
ReAct-style autonomous agents with tool usage
Multi-agent team coordination with leader pattern
Executable Python skills with YAML specifications
Interactive CLI with resource-action command structure
Python API for programmatic access
Multi-provider support (OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, Azure, OpenRouter, custom)
Conversation history tracking with SQLite
File attachment support for local and remote files
Glob pattern matching for file selection
Template variable substitution with ${variable} syntax
Structured JSON output with schema validation
Token usage tracking with cost estimation
Multiple LLM provider support with unified interface
Custom API endpoints and authentication
Generation configuration (temperature, max_tokens, etc.)
Conversation mode with automatic context preservation
Progress tracking for long-running operations
Comprehensive logging with rotation
HTTP API server with FastAPI
Web UI for browser-based access
Swagger documentation for API endpoints
Interactive curses-based chat UI
Safety settings and content filtering support

Complex multi-step AI workflows with dependency management
